verses.text: Receive not the grace of God in vain; 'now is the accepted time... the day of salvation.' Today. The time of mercy is now, not tomorrow.
Receive not the grace of God in vain; 'now is the accepted time... the day of salvation.' Today. The time of mercy is now, not tomorrow.
Giving no offence, we approve ourselves as ministers, in afflictions, stripes, and labours. Proven service. Their sufferings authenticate their ministry.
Giving no offence, we approve ourselves as ministers, in afflictions, stripes, and labours. Proven service. Their sufferings authenticate their ministry.
Giving no offence, we approve ourselves as ministers, in afflictions, stripes, and labours. Proven service. Their sufferings authenticate their ministry.
By pureness, knowledge, longsuffering, love, and 'the word of truth.' Marks of grace. A holy life adorns the message.
By pureness, knowledge, longsuffering, love, and 'the word of truth.' Marks of grace. A holy life adorns the message.
As deceivers, yet true; 'as dying, and, behold, we live'; sorrowful, yet rejoicing. Paradox of grace. The servant of God triumphs amid loss.
As deceivers, yet true; 'as dying, and, behold, we live'; sorrowful, yet rejoicing. Paradox of grace. The servant of God triumphs amid loss.
As deceivers, yet true; 'as dying, and, behold, we live'; sorrowful, yet rejoicing. Paradox of grace. The servant of God triumphs amid loss.
Our heart is enlarged toward you; be ye also enlarged. Open love. He asks his children to love him in return.
Our heart is enlarged toward you; be ye also enlarged. Open love. He asks his children to love him in return.
Our heart is enlarged toward you; be ye also enlarged. Open love. He asks his children to love him in return.
'Be ye not unequally yoked together with unbelievers'; what communion hath light with darkness? Be separate. The believer must not bind himself to the ungodly.
'Be ye not unequally yoked together with unbelievers'; what communion hath light with darkness? Be separate. The believer must not bind himself to the ungodly.
'Be ye not unequally yoked together with unbelievers'; what communion hath light with darkness? Be separate. The believer must not bind himself to the ungodly.
Come out and be separate; 'I will be a Father unto you, and ye shall be my sons and daughters.' Family of God. Separation from sin brings nearness to the Father.
